title: dodonaea ceratocarpa
intro: Dodonaea ceratocarpa, also known as the native hopbush, is a plant species native to Australia, belonging to the family Sapindaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, fever, and skin conditions due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The leaves and stems of Dodonaea ceratocarpa can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and poultices to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and skin irritations. In traditional Aboriginal medicine, the plant was used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, sore throats, and skin conditions.
